title: PCI-DSS 11.4 — Intrusion detection: IDS signature/health attestation + untuned alert monitoring
exclusions: Does NOT review alerts; attests the IDS/IPS sensors are healthy, up-to-date on signatures, and producing events.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_11_4_ids_attest' (every 30m) emits per sensor: sensor, last_seen, sig_version, age_d, state, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 11.4 Intrusion detection / prevention — directly evidenced.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disabling a sensor for 40m returns it with state=offline.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A healthy sensor with signatures ≤7d old is absent.

app: Splunk Add-on for Suricata, Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
| inputlookup pci_ids_sensors.csv
| join type=outer sensor
  [search index=ids earliest=-30m
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(sig_version) AS sig_version BY sensor]
| eval age_d=round((now()-strptime(sig_version,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| eval state=case(isnull(last_seen),"offline",last_seen<now()-1800,"stale",age_d>7,"signatures-stale",true(),"healthy")
| where state!="healthy"
| table sensor last_seen sig_version age_d state
description: PCI-DSS 11.4 requires IDS/IPS use. This UC attests the sensors are alive and their signatures are fresh. Sensor gaps are a top-3 audit finding.
value: Transforms annual sign-off into 30-minute ground-truth. IR team sees stale signatures before they become an audit issue.
implementation: (1) Maintain pci_ids_sensors.csv; (2) onboard sensor telemetry; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) non-healthy state opens a P1/P2; (5) monthly CISO review. Indexes required: `index=ids`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=palo:threat`, `sourcetype=snort:ids`, `sourcetype=suricata:eve`.
visualization: Donut of state, table of non-healthy sensors, single value 'stale sensors'.
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Suricata, Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ids` sourcetype IN (suricata:eve,snort:ids,palo:threat); sensor inventory `pci_ids_sensors.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain pci_ids_sensors.csv; (2) onboard sensor telemetry; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) non-healthy state opens a P1/P2; (5) monthly CISO review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup pci_ids_sensors.csv
| join type=outer sensor
  [search index=ids earliest=-30m
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(sig_version) AS sig_version BY sensor]
| eval age_d=round((now()-strptime(sig_version,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| eval state=case(isnull(last_seen),"offline",last_seen<now()-1800,"stale",age_d>7,"signatures-stale",true(),"healthy")
| where state!="healthy"
| table sensor last_seen sig_version age_d state
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 11.4 — Intrusion detection: IDS signature/health attestation + untuned alert monitoring** — Transforms annual sign-off into 30-minute ground-truth. IR team sees stale signatures before they become an audit issue.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ids` sourcetype IN (suricata:eve,snort:ids,palo:threat); sensor inventory `pci_ids_sensors.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Suricata, Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_d** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **state** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where state!="healthy"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_d, last_seen, sensor, sensor], sig_version, state.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Donut of state, table of non-healthy sensors, single value 'stale sensors'.
grandmaExplanation: We pCI-DSS 11.4 requires IDS/IPS use. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.Intrusion_Detection by Intrusion_Detection.signature, Intrusion_Detection.dest, Intrusion_Detection.severity | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Intrusion_Detection (CIM data model).
